data.title: EMD Serono to offer discounted fertility treatments in TrumpRx IVF program
data.description:
EMD Serono, the U.S. healthcare business of Germany's Merck KGaA (OTCPK:MKGAF), has brokered an agreement with the Trump administration to provide its in vitro fertilization at a deep discount to US consumers through the TrumpRx prescription drug program.

The company will provide direct-to-consumer purchasing for IVF therapies, including Gonal-f (follitropin alfa injection), Ovidrel (choriogonadotropin alfa injection), and Cetrotide (cetrorelix acetate for injection).

EMD Serono said that when the three drugs are used in a typical IVP protocol, women will save 84% off list prices.

In a fact sheet, the White House said that CMS estimates that women will save $2,200 per cycle of fertility drugs as a result of the arrangement, adding that the medications often account for ~20% of a IVF treatment cycle's cost.

EMD Serono will also file Pergoveris (follitropin alfa and lutropin alfa), which is already approved in Europe, for review by the US FDA under the National Priority Voucher program which cuts review time to 1-2 months from the usual 10-12. The White House called Pergoveris "a lower-cost alternative to expensive medications already on the U.S. market."

The administration added that EMD Serono will begin manufacturing its IVF drugs in the US. As a result, the company will not face potential pharma tariffs.

The president also unveiled a new benefit option for employers to offer fertility benefits directly to employees.

Separately, CVS Health (CVS) said it will be a "core partner" in the IVF drugs program through its CVS Specialty Pharmacy for those with insurance coverage for medications by providing pre-verification, claims support, and financial assistance.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: NextDecade makes positive final investment decision on Rio Grande LNG Train 5
data.description:
NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) +3.9% post-market Thursday after saying it reached a positive final investment decision on Train 5 of its Rio Grande liquefied natural gas export project in Texas, slightly more than a month after moving forward with a fourth train at the export terminal.

NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) also said the Train 5 FID came with a notice to proceed to Bechtel, the engineering, procurement and construction contractor for Rio Grande LNG.

Train 5 is commercially supported by 4.5 million metric tons/year of 20-year LNG agreements with JERA, EQT Corp., and ConocoPhillips; the planned date of the first commercial delivery from Train 5 is anticipated in H1 2031.

Train 5, which NextDecade (NEXT) expects will cost ~$6.7 billion to build, has expected LNG production capacity of ~6 million metric tons/year, bringing the total expected LNG production capacity under construction at Rio Grande LNG to ~30 million tons/year.

data.title: Rogers announces Armand Lauzon as new Chair of Board
data.description:
• Rogers (NYSE:ROG) announced on Thursday that Peter Wallace would be stepping down as the Chair of the Board. In anticipation, Armand Lauzon has been appointed as the next Chair effective immediately.

  • Armand Lauzon, with his expertise in strategy, operations, and governance and over 40 years of experience leading industrial and manufacturing companies through transformation and growth, would create long-term value for the shareholder.

  • The company also had plan to add a new independent director to the board. A director search is currently underway.

data.title: Gold hits another record with bank worries in focus alongside trade, rate cuts
data.description:
Gold and silver soared again to new all-time highs Thursday, sparked by concerns about credit quality in the economy, in addition to U.S.-China trade frictions and growing expectations for further interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.

Broader markets were hit as two major regional banks disclosed problems with loans involving allegations of fraud, adding to concerns about wider credit-related losses in the system, following the bankruptcies of two auto industry-related companies, which have helped boost demand for safe-havens such as gold and silver.

"When you see one cockroach, there are probably more," JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said on the bank’s earnings conference call earlier this week, referring to the collapse of First Brands and Tricolor Holdings.

Fed Chair Powell signaled this week that the Fed is on track to deliver another 25 basis point rate cut this month, and traders are betting on another reduction in December; non-yielding gold typically performs well in a low-rate environment.

Gold also has been supported by concerns over a resurgence in trade tensions, particularly between the U.S. and China.

The dollar continues to face headwinds of a stretched valuation and unsustainable U.S. fiscal policies, and investors seeking alternatives to the dollar are preferring gold over other developed market currencies, Insight Investment's Francesca Fornasari said in a note. which "likely reflects the challenging fiscal outlook for traditional safe-haven currencies like the Japanese yen and euro, as seen in France's budget issues" and concerns about the next leader of Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party.

"While comparisons are being made to the 1980s price peak, the current price rise is underpinned by structural drivers, indicating that elevated prices will likely sustain," according to analysts at Australian bank ANZ, which now forecast gold at $4,400/oz by year-end with a peak expected near $4,600/oz by June 2026.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for October delivery closed +2.5% to $4,280.20/oz, and front-month Comex October silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +3.8% to $53.023/oz, the fifth consecutive daily increases for both metals that have seen gold gain 8.4% and silver surge 13.2%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Liberty Energy Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.06 beats by $0.01, revenue of $947.4M misses by $17.4M
data.description:
• Liberty Energy press release (NYSE:LBRT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.06 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $947.4M (-8.9% Y/Y) misses by $17.4M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $128 million.
  • Distributed $13 million to shareholders through cash dividends.
  • Increased quarterly cash dividend by 13% to $0.09 per share beginning fourth quarter of 2025.
  • Shares -4.88% AH.

data.title: Go Residential Reit refinances Manhattan property, adds $64.5 million liquidity
data.description:
• GO Residential Real Estate Investment Trust (GO.U) said on Thursday that it has refinanced its One East River Place property in Manhattan, raising the mortgage principal to $251 million from $186.5 million.

  • The new five-year term loan adds about $64.5 million in proceeds, which the REIT used to partially repay its credit facility, currently bearing interest at SOFR plus 1.75%.
  • The move is expected to improve adjusted funds from operations per unit and bolster financial flexibility, the company said.
  • The base portion of the mortgage retains its prior rate until mid-2027, while the incremental amount carries a 5.02% rate through maturity in September 2030.
  • GO Residential said the refinancing extends its debt maturity profile and lowers near-term refinancing risk.
  • GO.U:CA closed -1.65% at $11.85.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CoreWeave appoints Jon Jones as chief revenue officer
data.description:
• CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) announced on Thursday the appointment of Jon Jones as its first chief revenue officer (CRO).
  • "Jon Jones will lead the company’s global revenue organization, with a focus on scaling products and sales for the next chapter of CoreWeave’s growth," said the company. 
  • Jon Jones has held roles from start-up founder to enterprise executive leadership. At Amazon, he recently served as Global Head of Startups and Venture Capital, and previously as Vice President of Go-to-Market, Products & Services.
  • CRWV -1.14% after hours to $140.12.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: McGraw Hill announces balance sheet optimization with $150M of debt reduction
data.description:
• McGraw Hill (NYSE:MH) announced on Thursday that it has successfully strengthened its balance sheet with a $150M prepayment of principal under its outstanding term loan facility. 
  • Following the latest paydown, the company has repaid $539M of term loan principal since its IPO, lowering the outstanding balance to around $618M.

data.title: Great Southern Bancorp signals stable loan balances and margin resilience amid competitive environment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Great Southern Bancorp, Inc. (GSBC) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Joe Turner, President and CEO, stated that "our third quarter results reflect the continued strength and consistency of our core banking fundamentals and a solid earnings performance in what remains a competitive and dynamic environment." He highlighted net income of $17.8 million for the quarter or $1.56 per diluted common share. Turner pointed to improved net interest income, no provision for credit losses, and continued management of noninterest expense as key drivers of year-over-year profit growth. He reported net interest income of $50.8 million for the quarter and noted, "Our annualized net interest margin improved to 3.72% from 3.42% a year ago, reflecting stable loan yields, disciplined asset liability management and effective funding cost control." Turner also shared that gross loans totaled $4.54 billion, a decline due to elevated commercial real estate and multifamily loan payoffs, and a reduction in construction loans as projects completed. He emphasized, "We remain focused on maintaining sound underwriting standards and disciplined credit practices... which have resulted in exceptional asset quality and negligible loan charge-offs."
  • Turner reported that total deposits decreased $77.5 million in the quarter, primarily in broker deposits, noting, "The deposit market remains highly competitive with sustained rate pressure in both core and broker deposit segments."
  • Turner underscored a continued focus on expense management, with noninterest expense at $36.1 million, and an efficiency ratio of 62.45%. He said, "We remain focused on maintaining strong positions related to credit quality, capital and liquidity."
  • Rex Copeland, Treasurer, provided further detail, stating, "We reported net income of $17.8 million or $1.56 per diluted common share in the third quarter of this year compared to $16.5 million or $1.41 per diluted common share in the third quarter of 2024 and $19.8 million or $1.72 per diluted common share in the second quarter of 2025." Copeland highlighted that the quarter-over-quarter decline was due to lower noninterest income and a modest increase in noninterest expense, with Q2 including significant nonrecurring income.

Outlook

  • Management expects overall loan balances to remain relatively stable through year-end, as stated by Copeland: "While overall loan balances are expected to remain relatively stable through year-end, the unfunded portion of construction and commercial loan commitments remain strong and reflecting steady borrower activity within our markets."
  • Turner indicated a focus on balancing loan growth with appropriate pricing and structure, saying, "Given our emphasis on balancing loan growth with appropriate pricing and loan structure, loan production in the quarter only partially offset the heightened payoff activity."
  • Copeland noted, "Future repricing opportunities will be closely monitored as market rates and deposit competition continue to evolve."

Financial Results

  • Net interest income was $50.8 million for the third quarter, compared to $51.0 million in Q2 2025 and $48 million in Q3 2024. Annualized net interest margin stood at 3.72%.
  • Total assets ended the quarter at $5.74 billion, down from $5.85 billion at June 30, 2025.
  • Total net loans, excluding mortgage loans held for sale, decreased to $4.47 billion at September 30, 2025, from $4.53 billion at June 30, 2025.
  • Noninterest income was $7.1 million for the quarter, compared to $8.2 million in the previous quarter, with commission income increasing by $206,000 year-over-year.
  • Noninterest expense totaled $36.1 million, up from $35.0 million in the previous quarter, driven by higher occupancy, equipment, salaries, professional fees, and other real estate owned expenses.
  • The efficiency ratio increased to 62.45% from 59.16% in Q2 2025.
  • The allowance for credit losses as a percentage of total loans was 1.43% at September 30, 2025, up from 1.41% at June 30, 2025.
  • Stockholders’ equity rose to $632.9 million, with a book value of $56.18 per common share.
  • The company repurchased 165,000 shares during the quarter and declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per common share.

Q&A

  • Damon Del Monte, Keefe, Bruyette, & Woods, Inc., asked about loan growth outlook and regional opportunities. Turner responded, "I think there's opportunity really in kind of every pocket of our footprint... It's just that payoffs are elevated as well."
  • Del Monte inquired about credit quality and potential portfolio weaknesses. Turner replied, "I couldn't say that we're seeing anything that broadly enough that you would say we're seeing general weakness... I would call that more idiosyncratic to that specific project as opposed to broader weakness."
  • Del Monte questioned the outlook for provision. Turner agreed with the analyst's assumption of minimal provision barring net charge-offs.
  • On rate sensitivity, Del Monte asked about further rate cuts' impact. Copeland said, "The rate cut that happened in September has not so far really impacted us... minor and spaced out rate cuts would really be too impactful probably."
  • John Rodis, Janney Montgomery Scott LLC, queried about operating expenses. Copeland said, "Some of the things in the occupancy category and equipment category... are probably kind of built in now as we've made some enhancements to systems and things of that nature." Turner added, "I think it was a pretty normal quarter from a noninterest expense standpoint."
  • Rodis also inquired about commission income levels. Copeland responded, "It's not a huge dollar amount... it's, what, $566,000 in the quarter... So I would say we're sort of at a higher level, like you said, than we've been for a while and whether that can be sustained, I can't honestly tell you for sure."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral tone, focusing on clarifying outlook for loan growth, credit quality, interest rate impacts, and expense trends.
  • Management’s tone during prepared remarks was confident, emphasizing sound credit and capital, but in Q&A, Turner and Copeland provided cautious, factual responses without overpromising on growth or income sustainability. Turner said, "I couldn't say that we're seeing anything that broadly enough that you would say we're seeing general weakness," reflecting steady but guarded confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was similarly steady, with slightly more emphasis on expense increases and competitive pressures. Analysts’ tone remained neutral, probing for sustainability and risk but not pressing aggressively.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Net income declined from $19.8 million in Q2 2025 to $17.8 million in Q3 2025, reflecting lower noninterest income and higher expenses.
  • Net interest margin improved to 3.72% from 3.68% in the previous quarter.
  • Noninterest expense increased by $1.1 million quarter-over-quarter, with the efficiency ratio rising accordingly.
  • Loan balances continued to contract, with ongoing elevated payoff activity noted in both quarters.
  • Management’s confidence in credit quality and capital strength persisted, but the end of benefit from the terminated swap was noted as a headwind for future quarters.
  • Analysts continued to focus on loan growth visibility, expense control, and margin sustainability, repeating questions from prior quarters about drivers and risks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Turner cited a highly competitive deposit market with sustained rate pressure as a challenge.
  • Copeland noted the loss of $2 million per quarter in interest income from the terminated swap as a factor for Q4 and beyond.
  • Elevated legal and professional fees, as well as increasing occupancy and technology expenses, contributed to higher noninterest expense.
  • Management highlighted continued monitoring of funding costs and repricing opportunities as market dynamics evolve.
  • No broad-based credit weaknesses were identified, though idiosyncratic risk in specific projects was acknowledged.

Final Takeaway

Management communicated continued stability in core banking performance, disciplined expense management, and strong asset quality despite a challenging and competitive deposit landscape. The company expects loan balances to remain stable through year-end, with careful attention to funding costs and margin preservation as the benefit from the terminated swap ends. Investors were reassured by consistent capital strength, prudent underwriting, and a continued commitment to operational efficiency, while also being cautioned about expense pressures and the evolving rate environment.
